name: PactFlow
what_it_does: Cloud-hosted Pact Broker for consumer-driven contract testing and API governance. Provides a REST API for managing contracts, broker configuration, webhooks, and CI/CD integration.
use_cases: Automated publishing and verification of Pact contracts in CI/CD pipelines
API compatibility gate checking before deployments
Managing contract versions and environments programmatically
Webhook-driven notifications on contract verification failures
Enterprise API governance with team-level access control
not_for: General API documentation or design (use Stoplight/Redocly)
Small teams that can self-host open-source Pact Broker
best_when: Agents need to query or update contract verification status in CI/CD pipelines, or enforce can-i-deploy gates before releasing services.
avoid_when: Your team doesn't practice consumer-driven contract testing, or you prefer self-hosted open-source Pact Broker.

auth.notes: API tokens scoped by team or read/write permission. Enterprise SSO via SAML. Token management via admin console.

pricing.notes: Developer plan free for up to 5 users. Enterprise pricing for SSO, audit logs, and dedicated infrastructure.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors follow HAL+JSON format consistent with REST; messages are functional but terse. Some edge cases underdocumented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Publishing contracts is idempotent by version — re-publishing same version/content is safe.
known_agent_gotchas: No MCP server — CI/CD integration via REST API or SDK only
Enterprise features (SSO, audit) require custom contract negotiation
HAL+JSON hypermedia format requires agents to follow links rather than construct URLs
Pact versioning scheme (provider/consumer/version) must be understood for correct API calls

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 and ISO27001 certified. API tokens with team-scoped permissions. SAML SSO on Enterprise. Audit logs available at Enterprise tier.
